things.name: Summerlee Museum of Scottish Industrial Life
things.description: Scotland's noisiest museum — working electric tramway, re-created mine, ironworks and cottages celebrating Lanarkshire's industrial heritage

things.name: Drumpellier Country Park
things.description: Popular country park with two lochs, woodland walks, visitor centre and a watersports facility on the edge of Coatbridge

things.name: Cairnhill Arts Centre
things.description: North Lanarkshire's arts hub housed in a Victorian mansion — galleries, theatre and community events in a leafy setting

things.name: Weavers' Cottage, Kilbarchan (NTS)
things.description: The last remaining weaver's cottage from the 18th-century weaving industry — a working loom demonstrates traditional Paisley pattern weaving

things.name: Bothwell Castle (Day Trip)
things.description: Finest 13th-century Scottish castle ruin — a massive circular keep and courtyard fortress on the high banks of the River Clyde

things.name: Caldercruix & Hillend Reservoir
things.description: Scenic reservoir walk and quiet village on the edge of North Lanarkshire — good birdwatching and a pleasant escape from the town

things.name: Glasgow (Day Trip)
things.description: Scotland's largest city just 30 minutes by train — world-class museums, food scene and the iconic Kelvingrove Gallery

things.name: Edinburgh (Day Trip)
things.description: Scotland's capital just 45 minutes away — Edinburgh Castle, the Royal Mile, National Museum and the vibrant New Town

things.name: Chatelherault Country Park (Day Trip)
things.description: William Adam hunting lodge in a spectacular country park above the River Avon gorge

things.name: New Lanark World Heritage Site (Day Trip)
things.description: UNESCO-listed Robert Owen mill village in the Clyde gorge — an inspiring vision of industrial social reform
